task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main workspace interface is visible, indicating a successful login (no login screen).

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the target channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active channel is '#release-updates', visible in the main chat header or highlighted in the left sidebar.

task_completion.description: Agent initiated the process to change the channel topic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Room Info' panel is open, the 'Edit Channel' modal is visible, or the agent is typing a command (like /topic) in the message input box.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the exact target topic text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Official Rocket.Chat release tracker - GitHub source: https://github.com/RocketChat/Rocket.Chat/releases' is visible in the topic input field of the edit modal/panel, or in the message input box.

task_completion.description: The channel topic was successfully saved and is visible in the UI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact target topic is displayed in the channel header (usually under the channel name '#release-updates'), in the Room Info panel, or a system message in the chat confirms the topic was changed to the target text.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser with the Rocket.Chat UI, not terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is entered into the actual Rocket.Chat input fields or modals, not just written in a text editor or a disconnected application.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) panel is not open in any of the screenshots.
